subject: Ecology
title: Forest Succession
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Forest Succession.

hook.text: After a wildfire clears a hillside, nature rebuilds the forest in a predictable sequence.

buildup.text: Grasses and herbs arrive first, stabilizing the soil and adding the first organic matter.

discovery.text: Fast-growing pioneer trees like birch and alder shade out grasses within a few years.

twist.text: Slowly, shade-tolerant species like oaks and maples replace the pioneers from below.

climax.text: A mature forest can take 200 years to develop—but disturbance resets the clock.

quiz.question: Which trees colonize first after a disturbance?
